automotive-ev-battery.title: Nissan collaborates with partners on sulphur-based solid-state battery research
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Nissan, the University of Oxford, and battery manufacturer Gelion have launched the research project CoRe-SoLiS to develop solid-state lithium-sulphur batteries with enhanced charging power, energy density, and lifespan. This collaboration represents a significant advancement in next-generation battery technology that could substantially improve EV performance and range.

automotive-ev-battery.title: T1 Energy buys KORE Power to cash in on the AI power boom
automotive-ev-battery.summary: T1 Energy, formerly FREYR Battery, has acquired battery storage and software company KORE Power to expand its presence in the rapidly growing energy storage and AI data center markets. This strategic acquisition positions the company to capitalize on surging demand for battery storage solutions driven by artificial intelligence infrastructure expansion.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Netherlands: Lidl to supply all 440 Stores with electric trucks by 2027
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Lidl has accelerated its electrification timeline, pledging to supply all 440 of its Dutch stores with electric trucks by 2027, three years ahead of the original target. This commitment demonstrates significant commercial momentum in heavy-duty vehicle electrification within European retail logistics.

automotive-ev-battery.title: B2U repurposes used Waymo batteries for second-life energy storage systems
automotive-ev-battery.summary: B2U Storage Solutions has partnered with Waymo to repurpose batteries from decommissioned robotaxi electric vehicles for second-life energy storage applications. This strategic collaboration addresses battery lifecycle sustainability while creating new revenue streams for used battery materials.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Audi unveils hybrid super sports car Nuvolari
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Audi has unveiled the limited-edition Nuvolari supercar, restricted to 499 units, combining a V8 biturbo engine with three electric motors to deliver 736 kW of system output, making it the brand's most powerful production vehicle. Deliveries are scheduled to begin in the first half of 2027.

automotive-ev-battery.title: EU's auto package: even higher electric quota for company cars?
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The EU's comprehensive automotive regulatory package remains under negotiation with finalization potentially delayed until 2027, as parliamentary members debate stricter electric vehicle quotas for company car fleets than those proposed by the European Commission. These regulatory developments will significantly impact EV adoption across European corporate transportation.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Wallbox deploys its first Supernova PowerRing DC fast chargers
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Wallbox has installed its inaugural Supernova PowerRing DC fast chargers at Port de Sitges, a major marina location on Spain's Mediterranean coast near Barcelona. This deployment marks an important milestone in the company's expansion of high-power public charging infrastructure.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Foxtron launches the Cavira electric SUV under its own brand
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Foxconn's automotive subsidiary Foxtron is launching the Cavira electric SUV in Taiwan, Australia, and New Zealand, with plans for additional market expansion. This marks the second production vehicle under Foxtron's own brand as the company transitions from contract manufacturing to original equipment manufacturing.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Prague has inaugurated a charging hub with 161 charging points
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Prague has transitioned its largest municipal charging hub into operational status in the ninth district, enabling simultaneous charging for up to 161 vehicles through a combination of AC chargers and Alpitronic Hyperchargers. The facility, operated by the municipal energy company PRE, represents significant infrastructure investment in urban EV charging capacity.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Chinese NEV Resale Value Surges 30%, Volume Up 29%
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese new energy vehicle resale values have increased 30 percent with corresponding 29 percent growth in transaction volumes, driven by expanding used EV market availability from BYD, Nio, and Denza brands. This trend indicates growing consumer confidence in NEV reliability and represents a maturing secondary market for electric vehicles in China.

tech-ai.title: Google will pay SpaceX $920M per month for compute
tech-ai.summary: Google has entered into a significant monthly payment agreement with SpaceX worth $920 million, driven by unexpectedly high demand for its recently launched AI products. The deal underscores the massive computational infrastructure investments required to support next-generation artificial intelligence services at scale.

tech-ai.title: Former cyber executive turned whistleblower accuses IBM of covering up several data breaches
tech-ai.summary: A lawsuit filed by a former cybersecurity executive alleges that IBM and its subsidiary companies were breached during the mid-2010s and that IBM failed to disclose and actively covered up the incidents. The allegations raise serious concerns about corporate security practices and transparency in the technology industry.

tech-ai.title: Reid Hoffman is leaving Microsoft's board to go 'founder mode' with startup Manus
tech-ai.summary: LinkedIn co-founder Reid Hoffman is stepping down from Microsoft's board after a decade to focus on Manus, his AI-focused drug discovery startup. The move reflects a significant leadership transition at Microsoft and demonstrates the continued appeal of AI-driven ventures to prominent technology investors.

tech-ai.title: Supabase doubles valuation to $10B in 8 months
tech-ai.summary: Open-source platform Supabase has achieved a $10 billion valuation in just eight months, benefiting significantly from AI coding tools such as Claude and Codex. The rapid growth exemplifies how AI-assisted development platforms are accelerating startup valuations in the modern software development landscape.

tech-ai.title: Google and FBI warn of ransomware group that sends fake IT workers to hack victims in person
tech-ai.summary: Law enforcement and Google have issued a joint warning about the Silent Ransom Group, a cybercriminal organization that sends impostors posing as IT support personnel to infiltrate company offices and steal data using USB drives or remote access tools. The warning highlights an emerging physical-digital hybrid threat to enterprise security.

tech-ai.title: GM's electric future depends on a new battery — and this building
tech-ai.summary: General Motors is accelerating its electric vehicle strategy by deploying new battery technology up to a year ahead of schedule to reduce EV costs and remain competitive. The company has invested in a dedicated facility as a cornerstone of this effort to democratize electric vehicle pricing.

tech-ai.title: Founders share VC horror stories, and some are naming names
tech-ai.summary: A viral conversation on X has emerged in which startup founders have publicly shared negative experiences with venture capital investors, with some explicitly naming individuals and firms. The discourse highlights ongoing tensions between founders and the venture capital community regarding ethics and conduct.

tech-ai.title: The most interesting startups right now want to get you off your phone
tech-ai.summary: While AI funding reaches record levels, a contrarian group of startups is emerging with products designed to reduce digital dependence, including Board, a platform focused on in-person gaming and social experiences, and DIY cyberdeck computers. These ventures represent an alternative movement within the startup ecosystem challenging the dominance of tech-maximalist solutions.

tech-ai.title: This is your laptop… on AI
tech-ai.summary: Major technology companies, particularly Nvidia, are promoting a vision of AI-integrated computing that would fundamentally change laptop design and usage patterns, with executives like Jensen Huang describing completely new computing paradigms. The industry-wide push raises questions about whether consumers and enterprises actually demand these AI-centric approaches.

tech-ai.title: As VC-backed e-bike startups went bankrupt, bootstrapped Lectric grew
tech-ai.summary: While venture-backed e-bike companies have failed, bootstrapped competitor Lectric has thrived and is expanding aggressively, having launched three new brands in the past six months. The company's success demonstrates that the crowded micromobility market remains viable for capital-efficient operators.

science-space.title: NASA's X-59 Aircraft Flies Supersonic for First Time
science-space.summary: NASA's experimental X-59 aircraft achieved a major milestone on June 5, flying faster than the speed of sound for the first time, reaching approximately Mach 1.1 (713 mph) at an altitude of 43,400 feet. The 81-minute test flight, piloted by Jim "Clue" Less at Edwards Air Force Base, sets the stage for demonstrating the aircraft's quiet supersonic capabilities later this year.

science-space.title: Scientists Find Wind Blowing From Our Milky Way's Black Hole After Half-Century Search
science-space.summary: After a 50-year search, astronomers have successfully detected powerful winds emanating from Sagittarius A*, the supermassive black hole at the center of the Milky Way galaxy. This discovery provides crucial insights into the dynamics and behavior of black holes and their impact on galactic environments.

science-space.title: First Precise Genome Editing of Human Embryos Triggers Praise and Alarm
science-space.summary: Scientists have achieved the first precise genome editing of human embryos, marking a significant breakthrough in genetic medicine that has generated both enthusiasm and ethical concerns within the scientific community. The development raises important questions about the future applications and regulatory oversight of human germline editing technology.

science-space.title: NASA's Artemis II Moon Mission Research Continues on Earth
science-space.summary: Since the Artemis II crew's safe return to Earth on April 10 following their record-setting lunar orbit mission, NASA science teams have been analyzing data and observations collected during the test flight. The research findings will provide critical information to support the safety and success of future human deep space exploration missions.

science-space.title: Astronauts on International Space Station Take Shelter in SpaceX Dragon as Cosmonauts Try to Fix Air Leak
science-space.summary: Astronauts aboard the International Space Station briefly sheltered in an attached SpaceX Dragon capsule on June 5 while Russian cosmonauts worked to repair a persistent air leak on the Russian segment of the orbiting laboratory. The incident highlights ongoing operational challenges and the importance of backup systems for crew safety.

science-space.title: NASA Announces Winners of 2026 University Innovation Competition
science-space.summary: NASA has announced the winners of its 2026 Revolutionary Aerospace Systems Concepts – Academic Linkage (RASC-AL) competition, with MIT's Exploration-Class Lunar Integrated Power System project earning first place honors. The competition challenges university students to develop innovative concepts and prototypes that address gaps in aerospace technology.

science-space.title: Astrophotographer Captures Colossal 'Godzilla' Plasma Cloud Stalking the Edge of the Sun
science-space.summary: An astrophotographer has captured remarkable backyard footage of a giant solar prominence and streams of coronal rain flowing along the sun's magnetic field lines, creating a spectacular display on the solar surface. The imagery provides insights into solar dynamics and the complex behavior of plasma in the sun's outer atmosphere.

science-space.title: $1.77 Trillion! SpaceX Is About to Become the 7th-Most Valuable American Company
science-space.summary: SpaceX is preparing for an initial public offering with share prices set at $135 each, yielding a valuation of $1.77 trillion and positioning the company to become the seventh-most valuable publicly traded company in the United States. The IPO reflects the growing significance of commercial spaceflight to the broader economy.

science-space.title: Colorful, Chaotic Jupiter
science-space.summary: NASA's Juno spacecraft captured a detailed, color-enhanced image of Jupiter's northern hemisphere during its 61st close flyby on May 12, 2024, processed by citizen scientist Gary Eason using raw JunoCam data. The enhanced image reveals the complex and dynamic cloud structures characteristic of the gas giant's chaotic atmosphere.

science-space.title: NASA Concludes Antenna Mishap Investigation, Releases Report
science-space.summary: NASA has completed its investigation into damage sustained at the 70-meter Deep Space Station 14 antenna at the Goldstone Deep Space Communications Complex and has classified the incident as a Type A mishap based on the total cost of damages. The agency has released a comprehensive report detailing the findings and planned repairs.

world-news.title: Iran war live: US strikes Iranian radar sites; Kuwait comes under attack
world-news.summary: The United States has conducted military strikes against Iranian radar installations while Kuwait reports coming under attack, marking a significant escalation in regional hostilities. The UN reports that 1.4 million people in Lebanon require humanitarian aid amid ongoing Israeli military operations in the country.

world-news.title: Putin says there is 'no point' meeting Zelensky over ending Ukraine war
world-news.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin has rejected an offer from Ukrainian President Zelensky for face-to-face diplomatic talks aimed at ending the ongoing war. The refusal represents a setback to peace negotiations and signals continued Russian intransigence on conflict resolution.

world-news.title: 'Pervasive fear' grips Gaza as Israeli attacks persist despite ceasefire
world-news.summary: Ongoing Israeli military operations continue to impact Gaza's civilian population despite ceasefire agreements, with a recent drone strike killing a young woman and injuring 15 others near Khan Younis. The attacks underscore the fragile humanitarian situation and sustained security threats in the territory.

world-news.title: France opens 'war crimes' probe into Israel's treatment of Gaza activists
world-news.summary: French authorities have initiated a war crimes investigation following allegations that Israeli forces abused and tortured French activists involved in a Gaza-bound humanitarian aid flotilla. The probe represents an escalation in international legal scrutiny of Israeli conduct.

world-news.title: Trump hails jobs surge, says Iran talks 'going well'
world-news.summary: The Trump administration reported positive economic indicators while indicating that diplomatic negotiations with Iran appear to be progressing favorably. The statement suggests potential movement toward resolving escalating tensions in the Middle East through diplomatic channels.

world-news.title: Ukraine strikes cargo ships and admits Romania drone blast
world-news.summary: Ukrainian forces have targeted five cargo ships in the Sea of Azov and Russian-occupied coastal waters carrying illegal cargo, while confirming responsibility for a drone strike affecting Romanian territory. The military operations reflect Ukraine's continued effort to disrupt Russian supply lines and logistics.

world-news.title: Trump makes pitch to farmers hard-hit by tariffs, high prices in Wisconsin
world-news.summary: The Trump administration is targeting rural voters struggling with the economic consequences of trade tariffs and regional warfare, seeking to consolidate political support ahead of upcoming elections. The outreach highlights the domestic political consequences of international economic and military conflicts.

world-news.title: Astronauts return to ISS after sheltering during air leak repair attempt
world-news.summary: Five astronauts aboard the International Space Station implemented emergency safety procedures after Russia attempted repairs to a tunnel section, highlighting ongoing technical challenges in the orbiting laboratory. The incident underscores international cooperation and safety protocols in space operations.

world-news.title: Iran footballers issued US visas for World Cup, says White House
world-news.summary: The United States has granted visas to Iranian national football team players ahead of their World Cup match against New Zealand in Los Angeles, representing a diplomatic gesture amid heightened regional tensions. The decision signals limited diplomatic engagement despite broader geopolitical conflict.

world-news.title: Gaza fishermen stay afloat with dinghies made from doorframes
world-news.summary: Fishermen in Gaza are constructing makeshift vessels from salvaged materials including fiberglass, wood, and doorframes recovered from conflict rubble, demonstrating the humanitarian challenges and civilian resilience amid ongoing military operations. The situation reflects the severe infrastructure damage and economic hardship affecting the territory's population.

finance-markets.title: Nasdaq tumbles 4% as shares in chip and memory groups sink
finance-markets.summary: The Nasdaq experienced a significant decline as technology and semiconductor stocks fell sharply, driven by rising expectations of a Federal Reserve rate increase. US bond yields surged following the market sell-off, reflecting growing expectations for tighter monetary policy.

finance-markets.title: Nasdaq 100 Sinks 5% in AI-Led Rout as Yields Climb: Markets Wrap
finance-markets.summary: Wall Street's technology-led rally halted as the Nasdaq 100 fell 5% amid a broad tech selloff and rising bond yields following a stronger-than-expected US jobs report. The market downturn reflects increased probability of a Federal Reserve rate hike in the near term.

finance-markets.title: Investors boost bets for Fed rate rise after bumper US jobs report
finance-markets.summary: The US economy added 172,000 jobs in May, signaling that the labour market is stabilizing and turning the corner. This robust jobs report has intensified investor expectations for a Federal Reserve rate increase in the coming months.

finance-markets.title: Meta weighs big equity raising after blockbuster Google deal
finance-markets.summary: Meta Platforms is considering a substantial equity offering of tens of billions of dollars to finance its artificial intelligence infrastructure investments. The move follows Meta's significant partnerships and reflects the tech sector's capital-intensive shift toward AI development.

finance-markets.title: SpaceX signs $30bn deal to lease computing capacity to Google
finance-markets.summary: SpaceX has secured a $30 billion agreement to provide computing capacity to Google, representing a major commercial partnership for the Musk-led conglomerate. The deal precedes SpaceX's anticipated initial public offering, which is expected to set records for the company's valuation.

finance-markets.title: Raizen Inks $13 Billion Out-of-Court Debt Deal With Creditors
finance-markets.summary: Raizen SA, a major Brazilian sugar and ethanol producer, has reached an out-of-court restructuring agreement with the majority of its creditors to rework approximately $13 billion in debt. The agreement represents a significant milestone in the company's financial recovery efforts.

finance-markets.title: Goldman's Flood Sees Buying Opportunity in Stock Market Selloff
finance-markets.summary: John Flood, head of Americas equities execution services at Goldman Sachs, characterizes Friday's market pullback as a buying opportunity rather than a cause for concern. Goldman maintains a constructive outlook with expectations for the S&P 500 to reach 8,000 by year-end.

finance-markets.title: Marvell Technology, Flex to Join S&P 500 Later This Month
finance-markets.summary: Marvell Technology Inc. and Flex Ltd. have been selected to join the S&P 500 index in the latest quarterly rebalance announced by S&P Dow Jones Indices. The inclusions reflect the companies' market capitalizations and prominence in the semiconductor and technology sectors.

finance-markets.title: Trump says US may take equity stakes in AI companies
finance-markets.summary: Former President Trump has suggested the US government could take equity stakes in artificial intelligence companies as part of a broader partnership strategy. The proposal aims to address voter concerns about AI technology ahead of political elections.

finance-markets.title: Canada's TerraVest Slides as Warrant Alleges Insider Tipping
finance-markets.summary: Canadian manufacturer TerraVest Industries experienced its steepest share price decline since 2011 following allegations of insider tipping involving its executive chairman. The warrant raises governance concerns and has significantly impacted investor confidence in the company.

crypto-web3.title: AI exposed a massive flaw in top crypto network and experts warn banks could be next
crypto-web3.summary: Security researchers have warned that an AI model uncovered a four-year-old critical vulnerability in Zcash, raising concerns about similar undiscovered bugs across cryptocurrency and traditional financial systems. The discovery highlights systemic security risks that extend beyond the crypto sector and could impact banking infrastructure.

crypto-web3.title: BlackRock-backed tokenization firm Securitize clears key hurdle to go public on NYSE
crypto-web3.summary: Securitize, the tokenization specialist behind BlackRock's BUIDL fund, has received SEC approval for its merger registration, clearing a major regulatory hurdle toward a NYSE listing under the ticker SECZ. The approval signals continued institutional adoption of blockchain-based asset tokenization.

crypto-web3.title: Zcash selloff extends past 50% amid bug disclosure as liquidations top $100 million
crypto-web3.summary: ZEC token crashed over 50 percent to approximately $310 following disclosure of a critical Orchard bug, with liquidations exceeding $116 million in 24 hours. The severe price collapse demonstrates the immediate market impact of disclosed security vulnerabilities in major cryptocurrency projects.

crypto-web3.title: U.S. House tax committee weighs crypto bills, including relief for small transactions
crypto-web3.summary: The House Ways and Means Committee has circulated seven draft bills ahead of a hearing next week, proposing tax relief measures for small-gain transactions, mining, and staking activities. These legislative efforts aim to reduce regulatory burdens on retail cryptocurrency users and participants.

crypto-web3.title: Alsobrooks says Clarity Act needs ethics deal before Senate vote
crypto-web3.summary: Maryland Democrat Senator Alsobrooks indicated that bipartisan crypto legislation is approaching completion but noted that outstanding concerns regarding ethics and illicit finance prevention must be resolved before a Senate vote. The statement suggests final negotiations on comprehensive digital asset regulatory framework.

crypto-web3.title: CME CEO Duffy says new perpetual futures could be 'disaster waiting to happen'
crypto-web3.summary: CME Group CEO Terry Duffy cautioned that newly approved U.S. perpetual futures contracts pose significant risks including retail investor losses and excessive leverage exposure. The warning raises regulatory concerns about derivatives market structure and risk management in cryptocurrency trading.

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin loses $60,000, falls to weakest price since October 2024
crypto-web3.summary: Bitcoin dropped below the $60,000 threshold to reach its lowest price since October 2024, driven by multiple concurrent headwinds including the largest institutional buyer turning seller, ETF investor outflows, and rising rate-hike expectations. The sharp decline reflects broader macroeconomic pressures on risk assets.

crypto-web3.title: Morgan Stanley lets clients lend bitcoin and other assets for in-kind spot crypto ETF conversions
crypto-web3.summary: Morgan Stanley Wealth Management has partnered with Galaxy Digital to enable eligible clients to lend cryptocurrency in exchange for cryptocurrency ETF shares through in-kind conversions. The initiative demonstrates growing institutional infrastructure supporting cryptocurrency integration into traditional wealth management services.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto Council for Innovation launches coalition to push for regulatory clarity on vaults
crypto-web3.summary: The Crypto Council for Innovation has established a new industry coalition focused on cryptocurrency vaults, an increasingly popular yield-generating mechanism for digital asset deposit and management. The coalition aims to secure regulatory clarity for vault products as adoption accelerates.

crypto-web3.title: U.S. job growth blows past forecasts, setting stage for Fed rate hikes
crypto-web3.summary: Stronger-than-expected U.S. employment data has increased the likelihood of Federal Reserve interest rate increases, creating additional headwinds for risk assets including cryptocurrency. The macroeconomic development contributes to broader market pressures affecting bitcoin and digital asset valuations.

startups-vc.title: Sector Snapshot: Defense Startup Funding Hits An All-Time Record As VCs Begin To Eye Exits
startups-vc.summary: Defense technology startup funding has reached an all-time record with over $14.6 billion in venture investment already in 2026, surpassing the previous annual record of $9.6 billion in 2025. This surge reflects growing investor confidence in the defense tech sector and signals emerging exit opportunities as the market matures.

startups-vc.title: Supabase doubles valuation to $10B in 8 months
startups-vc.summary: Supabase, an open-source backend-as-a-service platform, has achieved a $10 billion valuation in just eight months, demonstrating the market's strong appetite for developer infrastructure companies. The company's rapid growth has been fueled by increased adoption of AI-assisted coding tools among developers.

startups-vc.title: The Week's 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Megarounds Proliferate, Led By Enterprise Software, AI, And Space Tech
startups-vc.summary: This week's venture funding landscape continues to be dominated by mega-rounds exceeding $100 million, with enterprise software, artificial intelligence, and space technology companies leading capital deployment. The data provides a comprehensive overview of major funding activity and emerging sector trends in venture capital.

startups-vc.title: The 'together tech' wave might be the most intriguing startup bet of 2026
startups-vc.summary: A countertrend to AI-dominated startup funding is emerging, with founders building social and in-person experience platforms such as Board, a startup focused on games and community engagement. This movement reflects investor interest in companies addressing digital fatigue and promoting offline human connection.

startups-vc.title: Investors Have Poured Billions Into Plaintiff-Side Legal AI, But Defense Could Be The Next Big Opportunity
startups-vc.summary: While legal AI investment has heavily favored plaintiff-side applications, significant venture capital opportunities remain in defense-side legal technology platforms that offer litigation intelligence and risk benchmarking for corporate legal departments. This emerging segment represents an underexplored market expansion opportunity for legal tech startups.

startups-vc.title: SaaS Is Dead. Long Live SaaS! AI And The End Of The Rationing Of Knowledge Work
startups-vc.summary: Rather than signaling SaaS decline, artificial intelligence is enabling software companies to evolve from selling productivity tools to delivering direct knowledge-work outcomes, unlocking significant unmet demand. This transformation represents a fundamental expansion of the software market's total addressable opportunity.

startups-vc.title: Inside the AI Agent Stacks at SaaStr, Owner.com & Klaviyo | The Deep Dives From SaaStr AI 2026
startups-vc.summary: Leading B2B software companies have fundamentally restructured their operations and customer-facing products around AI agents rather than traditional human-centric workflows. These case studies illustrate the architectural and operational shifts required for high-growth AI-native companies.

startups-vc.title: $400M ARR With Under 200 People: What Lovable's Head of Growth Elena Verna Says Actually Works in B2B Now
startups-vc.summary: Lovable, a code-generation platform, has achieved $400 million annual recurring revenue with fewer than 200 employees by leveraging AI to automate software development. The company's scaling strategy addresses the challenge of maintaining competitive differentiation when AI democratizes core feature development.

startups-vc.title: How I Raised $14M For My Startup When I Stopped Pitching And Started Speaking
startups-vc.summary: A founder shares insights on raising $14 million by prioritizing authentic in-person relationships, thought leadership, and repeated investor engagement over aggressive sales-style fundraising approaches. The approach demonstrates the continued importance of personal networks and credibility in venture fundraising.

startups-vc.title: Defense tech, AI, and fundraising take center stage at StrictlyVC Los Angeles on June 18
startups-vc.summary: StrictlyVC is hosting an industry gathering on June 18 in Los Angeles featuring discussions on defense technology, artificial intelligence, and venture capital trends among investors, founders, and technology leaders. The event addresses critical shifts in capital deployment and emerging sectors of strategic importance.

health-biotech.title: CDC: Ebola outbreak in Central Africa could reach 20,000 cases without strong countermeasures
health-biotech.summary: U.S. analysis projects the Ebola outbreak in Central Africa could grow to 20,000 cases or more depending on the speed of patient isolation and outbreak response measures. The modeling underscores the critical importance of rapid intervention strategies to contain viral spread in the region.

health-biotech.title: Tiny HHS office tasked with protecting research participants' safety is running on fumes
health-biotech.summary: The Office for Human Research Protections (OHRP) has experienced an unprecedented loss of ethics expertise, raising serious concerns about its capacity to adequately safeguard research volunteers. The office's deteriorating resources threaten the integrity of the nation's research ethics oversight system.

health-biotech.title: #ADA26: Roche incretin shot works in mid-stage trial in type 1 diabetes
health-biotech.summary: Roche's daily incretin-based injection demonstrated efficacy in Phase 2 trials for type 1 diabetes, representing a potential breakthrough for a disease not typically responsive to GLP-1 therapies. The finding expands treatment options for patients with this insulin-dependent form of diabetes.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: What stripping civil service protections for thousands of federal workers will mean for HHS
health-biotech.summary: Thousands of HHS employees responsible for policy development have been reclassified to a designation allowing easier termination, raising concerns about regulatory continuity and institutional stability. The employment status changes could significantly impact the agency's ability to maintain consistent health policy implementation.

health-biotech.title: Innovent declares Phase 3 success for ADC that attracted huge Takeda deal
health-biotech.summary: Innovent Biologics' antibody-drug conjugate candidate successfully completed Phase 3 testing in advanced stomach cancer and is under priority review in China, supporting the company's significant deal with Takeda. The positive data demonstrates the therapeutic potential of the novel ADC approach in oncology.

health-biotech.title: Otsuka touts new IgAN evidence as it seeks full approval for Voyxact
health-biotech.summary: Otsuka Pharmaceutical released additional clinical evidence supporting full FDA approval of Voyxact, a monoclonal antibody for IgA nephropathy, a chronic autoimmune kidney disease. The new data strengthen the company's regulatory submission for this important therapeutic option.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Will Makary's FDA voucher program survive?
health-biotech.summary: The National Priority Voucher drug review program, initiated by former FDA Commissioner Marty Makary, faces uncertain viability amid ongoing industry concerns and regulatory questions. The program's future remains contingent on resolution of transparency and implementation issues.

health-biotech.title: Industry seeks more clarity on commissioner's voucher program
health-biotech.summary: Pharmaceutical companies and rare disease advocates expressed concerns about transparency and fairness in the FDA Commissioner's National Priority Voucher pilot program, questioning its structure and decision-making processes. Stakeholders are seeking clearer guidelines before broader program implementation.

health-biotech.title: House committee directs FDA to speed up IND sign-off process
health-biotech.summary: The House Appropriations Committee mandated FDA reforms to expedite clinical trial authorization processes, reflecting broader congressional efforts to ensure U.S. competitiveness with China and other nations in drug development. The directive addresses regulatory bottlenecks in early-stage trial approval.

health-biotech.title: Study data convince Lundbeck to push new migraine drug forward
health-biotech.summary: Lundbeck decided to advance development of a novel migraine therapeutic despite mid-stage trial results that fell short of some analyst expectations, indicating the drug's distinct mechanism offers clinical value. The decision reflects confidence in the compound's potential as an alternative to existing anti-CGRP treatments.

cybersecurity.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
cybersecurity.summary: A contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud account credentials and internal system details in a public GitHub repository until recently. Security experts characterized the leak as one of the most egregious government data breaches in recent history, containing sensitive files detailing CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes.

cybersecurity.title: IronWorm and New Miasma Worm Variant Hit npm in Supply Chain Attacks
cybersecurity.summary: Multiple software supply chain attacks have compromised the npm ecosystem, with attackers distributing a Rust-based information stealer and a self-spreading worm through poisoned versions of over 50 legitimate packages. The malware is designed to steal developer credentials and propagate across the software supply chain, posing significant risk to dependent projects.

cybersecurity.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster 'Dort' Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
cybersecurity.summary: Canadian authorities arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man suspected of operating Kimwolf, an Internet-of-Things botnet that compromised millions of devices for distributed denial-of-service attacks over six months. The suspect targeted a security researcher and journalist with DDoS, doxing, and swatting campaigns before facing criminal charges in both the U.S. and Canada.

cybersecurity.title: New Threat Cluster OP-512 Targets Microsoft IIS Servers with Custom Web Shell Framework
cybersecurity.summary: Researchers discovered a previously unreported threat cluster designated OP-512 actively targeting Microsoft Internet Information Services (IIS) servers to deploy a custom web shell framework for espionage purposes. ReliaQuest assessed with moderate to high confidence that the activity is linked to Chinese state-sponsored cyber operations.

cybersecurity.title: Hackers Exploit Critical Everest Forms Pro WordPress Plugin Flaw to Take Over Sites
cybersecurity.summary: Threat actors are actively exploiting CVE-2026-3300, a critical remote code execution vulnerability in the Everest Forms Pro WordPress plugin affecting approximately 4,000 installations. The flaw, with a CVSS score of 9.8, enables arbitrary code execution and complete site compromise across unpatched versions up to 1.9.12.

cybersecurity.title: PCPJack Hijacks 230 AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure Servers for Covert SMTP Relay Network
cybersecurity.summary: The threat actor PCPJack has compromised cloud servers across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure to establish a covert SMTP email relay network. Compromised business servers across North America, Europe, and Asia were converted into SMTP proxies and monitored every five minutes for relay capability distribution.

cybersecurity.title: Android Spyware Asin Targets Arabic Users via Fake News, PDF and War Map Apps
cybersecurity.summary: ESET researchers identified a new Android spyware called Asin targeting Arabic-speaking users through multiple campaigns initiated in early 2025. The malware is distributed via fraudulent applications masquerading as news utilities, war-related updates, and government news sources, including lookalike domains.

cybersecurity.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Scams Are Already Live: Fake Sites, Banking Malware, and Stolen Logins
cybersecurity.summary: Security researchers and the FBI warn that FIFA World Cup 2026-themed fraud campaigns are actively targeting fans ahead of the June 11 kickoff, featuring thousands of counterfeit FIFA domains and banking malware. Threat actors have successfully cloned FIFA's login pages to compromise legitimate user accounts and distribute malicious content through pirate streaming applications.

cybersecurity.title: China's TA4922 Expands Cybercrime Attacks Globally
cybersecurity.summary: One of the world's most diverse and least-focused cybercrime groups, TA4922, is expanding its attack footprint beyond East Asia into new geographic regions. The group's broadened operational scope represents an escalating threat to organizations across multiple continents.

cybersecurity.title: Adaptive, Agentic AI Worms Loom as Next Enterprise Threat
cybersecurity.summary: Cybersecurity researchers warn that agentic AI-powered worms capable of adapting to new environments and autonomously seeking vulnerabilities represent an emerging critical threat to enterprises. Security experts predict such adaptive threats will likely manifest within the next year, fundamentally changing the threat landscape.

energy-climate.title: DeBriefed 5 June 2026: UK eyes 2040 emissions cut | US 'dismantling' oceans research | China's solar slump
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief's weekly digest covers major climate and energy developments including the UK's consideration of a 2040 emissions reduction target, concerns about US ocean research cuts, and China's slowdown in solar expansion. These developments reflect shifting policy priorities and market dynamics across key global economies in the energy transition.

energy-climate.title: Data centres are becoming a defining force in power markets – can renewables keep up?
energy-climate.summary: Artificial intelligence-driven growth in data centre computing is creating unprecedented electricity demand that threatens to outpace renewable energy deployment capacity. The analysis examines whether the renewable energy sector can meet the scaling requirements of this emerging power market transformation.

energy-climate.title: Chart: Why China's solar boom is slowing down
energy-climate.summary: China's previously rapid solar power expansion is experiencing a significant slowdown despite remaining the world's largest solar market. The analysis explores the underlying factors constraining growth in what has been a cornerstone of global renewable energy development.

energy-climate.title: Q&A: The current state of 'carbon dioxide removal' around the world
energy-climate.summary: Carbon dioxide removal technologies are being positioned as essential tools for meeting climate targets, with deployment requirements accelerating beyond current trajectories. The Q&A examines the global status and future needs of CDR technology implementation.

energy-climate.title: Solar panel recycling yet to close cost gap before waste surge
energy-climate.summary: The economics of solar module recycling remain unviable compared to landfill disposal without mandatory extended producer responsibility policies, threatening significant waste management challenges as installations age. The sector faces a critical window to establish circular economy frameworks before the wave of end-of-life panels arrives.

energy-climate.title: Dutch solar owners asked to switch off during peak periods to ease distribution crisis
energy-climate.summary: Netherlands grid regulators are implementing new rules effective July 1 that will reduce priority for distributed solar generation during peak demand periods, marking a significant shift in grid access policies. The move reflects growing tensions between residential renewable deployment and distribution infrastructure capacity constraints.

energy-climate.title: US laboratory finds persistent ultraviolet light-induced degradation in solar modules
energy-climate.summary: The Renewable Energy Test Center's 2026 testing report reveals ongoing quality concerns with solar modules, including persistent ultraviolet-induced degradation and increased failures in damp heat and thermal cycling tests. While 19 manufacturers achieved high performance recognition, systemic reliability issues continue to threaten long-term system performance.

energy-climate.title: Chinese-Saudi joint venture to build 18GWh battery storage plant
energy-climate.summary: A new manufacturing facility combining Chinese and Saudi investment will produce 18 gigawatt-hours of battery storage capacity, with initial operations commencing in the first quarter of 2027. The project reflects growing international collaboration in energy storage infrastructure development to support renewable integration.

energy-climate.title: Drax signs 10-year PPA with Global Switch, attempts $755m Bluefield Solar purchase
energy-climate.summary: Drax Energy Solutions has secured a long-term corporate power purchase agreement with data centre operator Global Switch, expected to supply 30 percent of the company's electricity needs, while simultaneously pursuing a significant acquisition of a solar asset. The moves demonstrate corporate commitment to renewable energy procurement amid rising data centre demand.

energy-climate.title: Lithuania's grid operators can now disconnect solar plants without cybersecurity measures
energy-climate.summary: Lithuania's enhanced cybersecurity requirements for solar installations have created enforcement mechanisms allowing grid operators to disconnect non-compliant plants, though implementation has imposed costs and delays on plant owners. The policy reflects growing recognition of grid security risks in distributed renewable energy systems.

asia-pacific.title: Why North Korea’s Kim is doubling down on nuclear might as Xi visit looms
asia-pacific.summary:
North Korean ruler Kim Jong-un’s recent display of nuclear-processing capabilities appears timed with reports of an impending visit by China’s leader and fuelled by insecurities about Seoul’s nuclear submarine talks with Washington.
Analysts also say that mounting trilateral talks involving the United States, Japan and South Korea have galvanised Kim into doubling down on Pyongyang’s status as a nuclear-armed state.
The state-run Korean Central News Agency (KCNA) announced on Friday that Xi...

asia-pacific.title: Does Pete Hegseth’s volte-face on China reflect an America in decline?
asia-pacific.summary:
Listening to US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth at the recently concluded Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore, I thought of the iconic Sichuan opera act of face-changing, where performers switch masks in an instant.
Last year, Hegseth’s speech at the event was filled with blatant attacks on China. This year, he put on a completely different face, declaring that: “Under President Trump’s leadership, relations between the United States and China are better than they’ve been in many years.”
Why this...

asia-pacific.title: How everyday cooking oil is helping Japan fight an aviation fuel crisis
asia-pacific.summary:
Japanese homemaker Maki Watanabe carefully ⁠pours into a plastic bottle the oil she used to cook her ⁠deep-fried aubergines, doing her part in her Tokyo kitchen for a national effort to ramp up production of eco-friendly jet fuel.
“It would take a tremendous amount to make an aircraft fly, so I hope we can collect more,” said Watanabe, whose penchant for cooking allows her to donate about 40 litres (10 gallons) a year.
Her contribution is pooled at a nearby supermarket that is among roughly 300.

asia-pacific.title: Don’t dream it’s over: mainland students rethink Hong Kong over costs, culture
asia-pacific.summary:
Weeks before mainland Chinese student Carol Chen graduated from Baptist University in Hong Kong in July, the 22-year-old did the math and weighed the cost of living in the city against returning home.
Renting a room in the city would consume around half of the HK$20,000 (US$2,552) starting salary she expected as a junior data analyst.
“If I go back to Shanghai, I will only need to worry about daily expenses,” said Chen, a maths and statistics graduate.
Language barriers compounded the problem...

asia-pacific.title: China teacher under probe after using glue gun to scald little girl, resulting in blisters
asia-pacific.summary:
A kindergarten teacher in China has been suspended from her job and placed under police investigation for using a glue gun to scald a little girl’s lip, causing serious blisters.
The incident unfolded on the morning of May 27 in a nursery in Fengxian county, Jiangsu province, eastern China.
According to surveillance footage from the classroom, the woman children’s teacher let some children queue up to receive punishment one by one because she was angry that they were talking during class, the...

asia-pacific.title: US strikes Iranian radar sites after drone shootdown in Strait of Hormuz
asia-pacific.summary:
US forces struck Iranian coastal radar sites on Saturday after shooting down drones launched by Iran towards the Strait of Hormuz, the US military said, in the latest escalation complicating efforts to end the war between the two countries.
The US military believed the four Iranian drones were targeting regional maritime traffic, an official said.
US Central Command said on social media that the US then struck Iran’s surveillance ‌sites in Goruk and Qeshm Island, which are both on the Strait of.

asia-pacific.title: Hong Kong parents of undocumented baby Danny confident of regaining custody
asia-pacific.summary:
The Hong Kong parents of undocumented baby Danny have expressed confidence that they can obtain legal guardianship of their son, who is now in a care home, as immigration authorities could process his birth certificate as early as Saturday.
The unmarried couple also said they saw a “glimmer of hope” in resolving the custody case of their four-year-old daughter Lily, whom Swedish authorities have taken into care over child welfare concerns.
“We believe the social workers will handle the matter in

asia-pacific.title: Targeting US$610 billion longevity market, biotech firms race to reverse ageing
asia-pacific.summary:
For Beijing-based METiS TechBio CEO Lai Tsai-ta, ageing is comparable to bugs building up in a complex software system – it happens once errors begin to accumulate in the genetic code of human cells, such as their DNA sequences.
“Those errors can be reprogrammed. It becomes possible to use AI to read, rewrite and reverse cells, or at least slow the ageing process,” Lai said in an interview with the South China Morning Post.
The process could begin by fixing “immune cells, such as T cells, as...

asia-pacific.title: How Malaysia’s Langkawi became a paradise for smugglers
asia-pacific.summary:
Waves break along Cenang Beach as the sun drops over the Andaman Sea, washing Langkawi’s white sand in gold. Tourists sip fresh coconuts beneath rows of bright umbrellas, gazing out at the “Jewel of Kedah”: a duty-free archipelago long sold as one of Malaysia’s premier tropical escapes.
Out on the water, meanwhile, small boats traverse the 8km (five miles) of open water that separates Langkawi from Thailand’s Koh Tarutao. On fast boats, the crossing takes a matter of minutes. No navigation...

asia-pacific.title: Putin says no reason to meet Ukraine’s Zelensky, calls his letter ‘rude’
asia-pacific.summary:
Russian President Vladimir Putin said on Friday he currently saw no reason to meet Volodymyr Zelensky after the Ukrainian president published an open letter proposing they hold face-to-face talks to agree an end to a war now in its fifth year.
In his letter, which was sent to other countries, including the US, Zelensky said most Russians ‌had grown tired of Ukrainian missile and drone attacks, high inflation and fuel shortages, and were ready for peace.
He also suggested that continuing the war.
